title: Tender Shuttle: Single Tender Sustained Flow

scene.description: Engine 181 is operating at a rural structure fire with no hydrants available. Engine 181 is drafting from a portable tank. Tender 184 has a 2,500-gallon tank. Use 90% usable tank capacity. Fill time is 4 minutes, travel to the scene is 6 minutes, dump time is 3 minutes, and return travel is 6 minutes. What sustained water supply in GPM can this single-tender shuttle provide?

problems.title: Tender Shuttle: Single Tender Sustained Flow
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a rural structure fire with no hydrants available. Engine 181 is drafting from a portable tank. Tender 184 has a 2,500-gallon tank. Use 90% usable tank capacity. Fill time is 4 minutes, travel to the scene is 6 minutes, dump time is 3 minutes, and return travel is 6 minutes. What sustained water supply in GPM can this single-tender shuttle provide?
formulaBreakdown: This is a water-supply scenario. The final answer is sustained shuttle flow in GPM, not pump pressure.
Tender Shuttle GPM = usable water delivered per trip ÷ total shuttle cycle time in minutes.
Usable water = 2,500 gallons × 0.90 = 2,250 gallons.
Cycle time = fill time + travel to scene + dump time + return travel.
Cycle time = 4 + 6 + 3 + 6 = 19 minutes.
Sustained flow = 2,250 ÷ 19 = 118.4 GPM.
Round to 120 GPM.
Pump pressure fields are 0 because this scenario is not asking for pump discharge pressure.
problems.instructorExplanation: This problem estimates sustained water supply from a single tender shuttle by dividing usable gallons per trip by the full round-trip cycle time.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes: using full tank capacity instead of usable water, forgetting fill time, forgetting dump time, only counting one-way travel, or calculating gallons per trip but not dividing by cycle time.

problems.title: Tender Shuttle: 3,000-Gallon Tender, 19-Minute Cycle
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a rural structure fire with no hydrants available and is drafting from a portable tank. Tender 184 has a 3,000-gallon tank. Use 90% usable tank capacity. Fill time is 4 minutes, travel to the scene is 6 minutes, dump time is 3 minutes, and return travel is 6 minutes. What sustained water supply in GPM can this single-tender shuttle provide?
formulaBreakdown: Tender Shuttle GPM = usable water delivered per trip ÷ total shuttle cycle time.
Usable water = 3,000 gallons × 0.90 = 2,700 gallons.
Cycle time = fill 4 + travel to scene 6 + dump 3 + return 6 = 19 minutes.
Sustained flow = 2,700 ÷ 19 = 142.1 GPM.
Rounded answer = 140 GPM.
Pump pressure fields are 0 because this scenario asks for sustained water supply, not PDP.
problems.instructorExplanation: This problem estimates sustained water supply from a single tender shuttle by dividing usable gallons per trip by the full round-trip cycle time.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes: using full tank capacity instead of usable water, forgetting fill time, forgetting dump time, only counting one-way travel, or calculating gallons per trip but not dividing by cycle time.

problems.title: Tender Shuttle: 2,500-Gallon Tender, 21-Minute Cycle
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating at a rural structure fire with no hydrants available and is drafting from a portable tank. Tender 184 has a 2,500-gallon tank. Use 90% usable tank capacity. Fill time is 4 minutes, travel to the scene is 8 minutes, dump time is 3 minutes, and return travel is 6 minutes. What sustained water supply in GPM can this single-tender shuttle provide?
formulaBreakdown: Tender Shuttle GPM = usable water delivered per trip ÷ total shuttle cycle time.
Usable water = 2,500 gallons × 0.90 = 2,250 gallons.
Cycle time = fill 4 + travel to scene 8 + dump 3 + return 6 = 21 minutes.
Sustained flow = 2,250 ÷ 21 = 107.1 GPM.
Rounded answer = 105 GPM.
Pump pressure fields are 0 because this scenario asks for sustained water supply, not PDP.
problems.instructorExplanation: This problem estimates sustained water supply from a single tender shuttle by dividing usable gallons per trip by the full round-trip cycle time.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes: using full tank capacity instead of usable water, forgetting fill time, forgetting dump time, only counting one-way travel, or calculating gallons per trip but not dividing by cycle time.

Cycle time = fill 4 + travel to scene 6 + dump 5 + return 6 = 21 minutes.
Sustained flow = 2,250 ÷ 21 = 107.1 GPM.
Rounded answer = 105 GPM.
Pump pressure fields are 0 because this scenario asks for sustained water supply, not PDP.
